summary: The diff implements both REQ-36 (backend authorization fix) and REQ-37 (frontend authenticated actor guard). REQ-37 appears fully addressed with the identity check and toast messages in useQueries.ts and SiteSettingsSection.tsx. However, REQ-36 has a critical gap: the backend main.mo does not show a system func init or postupgrade hook that captures the deployer's principal at canister install time and stores it as the initial admin in stable state. Instead, it uses an AccessControl/MixinAuthorization abstraction whose initialization logic is not shown.

missing_requirements.requirement: Capture the deployer's principal at canister install time (using the install message caller) and store it as the initial admin in stable state via system func init or postupgrade.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo diff shows no system func init or system func postupgrade function that captures the install-time caller and seeds the admin list. The AccessControl.initState() call is present but its internals are not shown — there is no visible mechanism to record the deploying principal at install time from the actor level.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) that renames the stable variable from siteSettingsPersistent to siteSettings via a Migration.run migration function applied with the with-migration actor attribute.
hallucinated_features.reason: Neither REQ-36 nor REQ-37 requested any stable variable renaming or canister data migration. The build request only asked for authorization logic fixes and frontend actor guarding.
